machine.name: Home Medication Cost Barrier Monitor
machine.description: Monitors prescription fill rates, dose adherence, cost-driven non-adherence, and generic/assistance program access for low- and medium-income adults managing chronic conditions at home. Medication cost barriers are a leading driver of avoidable hospitalization and disease progression in economically vulnerable populations. Output at [2011:2015] feeds the Home Social Isolation Monitor, capturing how pharmacy access barriers compound transportation and social barriers.

metadata.domain: Home Health — Medication Access / Cost Barriers
metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.populationFocus: Adults on chronic medications; uninsured or underinsured patients; Medicare Part D gap patients; households below 200% FPL

sensorNormalization.fill_ratio_norm: 0.0=no prescriptions filled (complete non-adherence), 0.5=~50% of prescribed fills completed, 1.0=all prescriptions filled on schedule
sensorNormalization.dose_adherence_norm: 0.0=not taking medications (stopped or skipping >50% doses), 0.5=taking but rationing (splitting doses, skipping days), 1.0=full prescribed adherence
sensorNormalization.cost_barrier_norm: 0.0=cost explicitly cited as reason for not filling (unaffordable even with insurance), 0.5=difficulty but managing with assistance, 1.0=no cost barrier reported
sensorNormalization.generic_access_norm: 0.0=no access to generic or assistance programs; paying full brand price or going without, 0.5=partial program access (copay cards, one drug covered), 1.0=fully enrolled in PAP or full generic coverage

governance.notes: Auto-backfilled by scripts/backfill-governance.mjs from primaryDomain="health-personal".  Review and tune the routing as the team owning HomeMedicationCostBarrierMonitor matures.

rules.description: ADHERENCE_CRISIS: complete medication non-adherence due to cost barrier at [2011:2015]
COST_BARRIER_ACTIVE: ongoing cost barrier to prescribed medications at [2011:2015]
MEDICATION_MANAGED: prescription access and adherence adequate at [2011:2015]

sequences.name: Adherence Collapse: ADHERENT+ → RATIONING → NON_ADHERENT → ADHERENCE_CRISIS

metadata.description: Tracks cost-driven medication adherence collapse. Patients who cannot afford medications first ration (splitting doses, skipping days), then stop refilling entirely. This sequence requires a confirmed ADHERENT baseline before the crisis escalation can arm — preventing false flags on patients who were never on medications.

metadata.name: ADHERENT
metadata.description: ADHERENT+ — prescriptions being filled; doses taken as prescribed; cost barriers manageable; generics or assistance covering most needs. isInitial A*A = A+.

metadata.name: RATIONING
metadata.description: RATIONING — fill rate dropping; patient splitting doses or skipping days; cost barrier explicitly reported.

metadata.name: NON_ADHERENT
metadata.description: NON_ADHERENT — prescriptions not being filled; most or all doses missed; cost barrier unresolved; no assistance program active. Fires ADHERENCE_CRISIS.
ADHERENCE_CRISIS: complete medication non-adherence due to cost barrier at [2011:2015]
metadata.action: Patient assistance program enrollment; 340B pharmacy referral; social worker outreach; emergency supply bridge

metadata.description: Fires COST_BARRIER_ACTIVE when patient reports consistent cost barriers to medication access but has not yet reached full non-adherence. Targets the working-poor population managing chronic conditions on bare minimum insurance coverage or no coverage — the largest unserved medication access group.

metadata.name: STRUGGLING
metadata.description: STRUGGLING+ — partial fill rate; some adherence; cost barrier present with no active assistance program. isInitial A*A = A+: fires COST_BARRIER_ACTIVE each matching cycle.
metadata.scenario: Patient buying insulin at Walmart; skipping cholesterol med to afford blood pressure medication

metadata.description: COST_BARRIER_ACTIVE: ongoing cost barrier to prescribed medications at [2011:2015]
metadata.action: Pharmacy assistance program review; GoodRx/NeedyMeds screening; formulary substitution review

metadata.description: Fires MEDICATION_MANAGED when fill rates are adequate, adherence confirmed, and cost barriers resolved through generics, assistance programs, or insurance coverage. Represents successful pharmacy access navigation for a low-income patient.

metadata.name: MANAGED
metadata.description: MANAGED+ — all prescriptions filled; adherence confirmed; cost barriers resolved via generics or assistance programs. Fires MEDICATION_MANAGED each matching cycle.
metadata.scenario: Patient enrolled in Medicaid managed care with generic coverage; or enrolled in manufacturer PAP

metadata.description: MEDICATION_MANAGED: prescription access and adherence adequate at [2011:2015]
metadata.action: Social isolation monitor registers medication access stable

inputSequences.name: Medicare Part D Donut Hole Gap
inputSequences.description: Senior hits Medicare Part D coverage gap in August; begins rationing insulin; home health nurse identifies crisis; Low Income Subsidy enrollment resolves gap.
